heading: Interstate bank mergers
label: 1831u Interstate bank mergers

children.heading: Approval of interstate merger transactions authorized
children.label: a Approval of interstate merger transactions authorized

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Beginning on , the responsible agency may approve a merger transaction under between insured banks with different home States, without regard to whether such transaction is prohibited under the law of any State.June 1, 1997section 1828(c) of this title

children.heading: State election to prohibit interstate merger transactions
children.label: 2 State election to prohibit interstate merger transactions
i
children.text: applies equally to all out-of-State banks; and

children.label: ii
children.text: expressly prohibits merger transactions involving out-of-State banks.

children.heading: No effect on prior approvals of merger transactions
children.label: B No effect on prior approvals of merger transactions
children.text: A law enacted by a State pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall have no effect on merger transactions that were approved before the effective date of such law.

children.heading: State election to permit early interstate merger transactions
children.label: 3 State election to permit early interstate merger transactions
i
children.text: applies equally to all out-of-State banks; and

children.label: ii
children.text: expressly permits interstate merger transactions with all out-of-State banks.

children.label: i
children.text: the conditions do not have the effect of discriminating against out-of-State banks, out-of-State bank holding companies, or any subsidiary of such bank or company (other than on the basis of a nationwide reciprocal treatment requirement);

children.label: ii
children.text: the imposition of the conditions is not preempted by Federal law; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the conditions do not apply or require performance after .May 31, 1997

children.heading: Interstate merger transactions involving acquisitions of branches
children.label: 4 Interstate merger transactions involving acquisitions of branches

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An interstate merger transaction may involve the acquisition of a branch of an insured bank without the acquisition of the bank only if the law of the State in which the branch is located permits out-of-State banks to acquire a branch of a bank in such State without acquiring the bank.

children.heading: Treatment of branch for purposes of this section
children.label: B Treatment of branch for purposes of this section
children.text: In the case of an interstate merger transaction which involves the acquisition of a branch of an insured bank without the acquisition of the bank, the branch shall be treated, for purposes of this section, as an insured bank the home State of which is the State in which the branch is located.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The responsible agency may not approve an application pursuant to paragraph (1) that would have the effect of permitting an out-of-State bank or out-of-State bank holding company to acquire a bank in a host State that has not been in existence for the minimum period of time, if any, specified in the statutory law of the host State.

children.heading: Special rule for State age laws specifying a period of more than 5 years
children.label: B Special rule for State age laws specifying a period of more than 5 years
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the responsible agency may approve a merger transaction pursuant to paragraph (1) involving the acquisition of a bank that has been in existence at least 5 years without regard to any longer minimum period of time specified in a statutory law of the host State.

children.heading: Shell banks
children.label: 6 Shell banks
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a bank that has been chartered solely for the purpose of, and does not open for business prior to, acquiring control of, or acquiring all or substantially all of the assets of, an existing bank or branch shall be deemed to have been in existence for the same period of time as the bank or branch to be acquired.

children.heading: Provisions relating to application and approval process
children.label: b Provisions relating to application and approval process
I
children.text: does not have the effect of discriminating against out-of-State banks or out-of-State bank holding companies or subsidiaries of such banks or bank holding companies; and

children.label: II
children.text: is similar in effect to any requirement imposed by the host State on a nonbanking corporation incorporated in another State that engages in business in the host State; and

children.label: ii
children.text: submit a copy of the application to the State bank supervisor of the host State.

children.heading: Penalty for failure to comply
children.label: B Penalty for failure to comply
children.text: The responsible agency may not approve an application for an interstate merger transaction if the applicant materially fails to comply with subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Nationwide concentration limits
children.label: A Nationwide concentration limits
children.text: The responsible agency may not approve an application for an interstate merger transaction if the resulting bank (including all insured depository institutions which are affiliates of the resulting bank), upon consummation of the transaction, would control more than 10 percent of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the United States.

children.heading: Statewide concentration limits other than with respect to initial entries
children.label: B Statewide concentration limits other than with respect to initial entries
i
children.text: any bank involved in the transaction (including all insured depository institutions which are affiliates of any such bank) has a branch in any State in which any other bank involved in the transaction has a branch; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the resulting bank (including all insured depository institutions which would be affiliates of the resulting bank), upon consummation of the transaction, would control 30 percent or more of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in any such State.

children.heading: Effectiveness of State deposit caps
children.label: C Effectiveness of State deposit caps
children.text: No provision of this subsection shall be construed as affecting the authority of any State to limit, by statute, regulation, or order, the percentage of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the State which may be held or controlled by any bank or bank holding company (including all insured depository institutions which are affiliates of the bank or bank holding company) to the extent the application of such limitation does not discriminate against out-of-State banks, out-of-State bank holding companies, or subsidiaries of such banks or holding companies.

children.label: i
children.text: there is a limitation described in subparagraph (C) in a State statute, regulation, or order which has the effect of permitting a bank or bank holding company (including all insured depository institutions which are affiliates of the bank or bank holding company) to control a greater percentage of total deposits of all insured depository institutions in the State than the percentage permitted under subparagraph (B); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the transaction is approved by the appropriate State bank supervisor of such State and the standard on which such approval is based does not have the effect of discriminating against out-of-State banks, out-of-State bank holding companies, or subsidiaries of such banks or holding companies.

children.heading: Exception for certain banks
children.label: E Exception for certain banks
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply with respect to any interstate merger transaction involving only affiliated banks.

children.label: A
children.text: comply with the responsibilities of the agency regarding such application under ;section 2903 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: take into account the most recent written evaluation under of any bank which would be an affiliate of the resulting bank; andsection 2903 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: take into account the record of compliance of any applicant bank with applicable State community reinvestment laws.

children.heading: Adequacy of capital and management skills
children.label: 4 Adequacy of capital and management skills
A
children.text: each bank involved in the transaction is adequately capitalized as of the date the application is filed; and

children.label: B
children.text: the responsible agency determines that the resulting bank will be well capitalized and well managed upon the consummation of the transaction.

children.heading: Surrender of charter after merger transaction
children.label: 5 Surrender of charter after merger transaction
children.text: The charters of all banks involved in an interstate merger transaction, other than the charter of the resulting bank, shall be surrendered, upon request, to the Federal banking agency or State bank supervisor which issued the charter.

children.heading: Applicability of certain laws to interstate banking operations
children.label: c Applicability of certain laws to interstate banking operations

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No provision of this section shall be construed as affecting the authority of any State or political subdivision of any State to adopt, apply, or administer any tax or method of taxation to any bank, bank holding company, or foreign bank, or any affiliate of any bank, bank holding company, or foreign bank, to the extent such tax or tax method is otherwise permissible by or under the Constitution of the United States or other Federal law.

children.heading: Imposition of shares tax by host States
children.label: B Imposition of shares tax by host States
children.text: In the case of a branch of an out-of-State bank which results from an interstate merger transaction, a proportionate amount of the value of the shares of the out-of-State bank may be subject to any bank shares tax levied or imposed by the host State, or any political subdivision of such host State that imposes such tax based upon a method adopted by the host State, which may include allocation and apportionment.

children.label: A
children.text: the applicability of the antitrust laws; or

children.label: B
children.text: the applicability, if any, of any State law which is similar to the antitrust laws.

children.heading: Reservation of certain rights to States
children.label: 3 Reservation of certain rights to States
A
children.text: determine the authority of State banks chartered by that State to establish and maintain branches; or

children.label: B
children.text: supervise, regulate, and examine State banks chartered by that State.

children.label: A
children.text: does not discriminate against out-of-State banks or bank holding companies; and

children.label: B
children.text: is not preempted by any Federal law regarding the same subject.

children.heading: Operations of the resulting bank
children.label: d Operations of the resulting bank

children.heading: Continued operations
children.label: 1 Continued operations
children.text: A resulting bank may, subject to the approval of the appropriate Federal banking agency, retain and operate, as a main office or a branch, any office that any bank involved in an interstate merger transaction was operating as a main office or a branch immediately before the merger transaction.

children.heading: Additional branches
children.label: 2 Additional branches
children.text: Following the consummation of any interstate merger transaction, the resulting bank may establish, acquire, or operate additional branches at any location where any bank involved in the transaction could have established, acquired, or operated a branch under applicable Federal or State law if such bank had not been a party to the merger transaction.

children.label: A
children.text: the home State of the acquired bank imposed conditions on such acquisition by such out-of-State bank holding company; or

children.label: B
children.text: the bank holding company made commitments to such State in connection with the acquisition,

children.heading: Exception for banks in default or in danger of default
children.label: e Exception for banks in default or in danger of default
children.text: If an application under subsection (a)(1) for approval of a merger transaction which involves 1 or more banks in default or in danger of default or with respect to which the Corporation provides assistance under , the responsible agency may approve such application without regard to subsection (b), or paragraph (2), (4), or (5) of subsection (a).section 1823(c) of this title

children.heading: Applicable rate and other charge limitations
children.label: f Applicable rate and other charge limitations
i
children.text: the maximum interest rate or amount of interest, discount points, finance charges, or other similar charges that may be charged, taken, received, or reserved in a similar transaction under the constitution or any statute or other law of the home State of the out-of-State insured depository institution establishing any such branch, without reference to this section, as such maximum interest rate or amount of interest may change from time to time; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the maximum rate or amount of interest, discount points, finance charges, or other similar charges that may be charged, taken, received, or reserved in a similar transaction by a State insured depository institution chartered under the laws of such State or a national bank or Federal savings association whose main office is located in such State without reference to this section; and

children.label: I
children.text: uniform accessibility of student loans, including the issuance of qualified student loan bonds as set forth in ;section 144(b) of title 26

children.label: II
children.text: the uniform accessibility of mortgage loans, including the issuance of qualified mortgage bonds and qualified veterans’ mortgage bonds as set forth in section 143 of such title;

children.label: aa
children.text: the issuance of exempt facility bonds for qualified residential rental property as set forth in section 142(d) of such title; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the issuance of low income housing tax credits as set forth in section 42 of such title; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the uniform accessibility of bonds and obligations issued under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009;

children.label: ii
children.text: to facilitate interstate commerce through the issuance of bonds and obligations under any provision of State law, including bonds and obligations for the purpose of economic development, education, and improvements to infrastructure; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to facilitate interstate commerce generally, including consumer loans, in the case of any person or governmental entity (other than a depository institution subject to subparagraph (A) and paragraph (2)).

children.label: i
children.text: the authority of any insured depository institution to take, receive, reserve, and charge interest on any loan made in any State other than the State referred to in paragraph (1); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the applicability of , , or .section 1735f–7a of this titlesection 85 of this titlesection 1831d of this title

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: B Applicability
children.text: This subsection shall be construed to apply to any loan or discount made, or note, bill of exchange, financing transaction, or other evidence of debt, originated by an insured depository institution, a governmental entity located in such State, or a person that is not a depository institution described in subparagraph (A) doing business in such State.

children.heading: Adequately capitalized
children.label: 1 Adequately capitalized
children.text: The term “adequately capitalized” has the same meaning as in section 1831 of this title.o

children.label: A
children.text: has the same meaning as in subsection (a) of ; andsection 12 of title 15

children.label: B
children.text: includes to the extent such section 45 relates to unfair methods of competition.section 45 of title 15

children.heading: Branch
children.label: 3 Branch
children.text: The term “branch” means any domestic branch.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to a national bank, the State in which the main office of the bank is located; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to a State bank, the State by which the bank is chartered; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to a bank holding company, has the same meaning as in section 1841()(4) of this title.o

children.heading: Host State
children.label: 5 Host State
children.text: The term “host State” means, with respect to a bank, a State, other than the home State of the bank, in which the bank maintains, or seeks to establish and maintain, a branch.

children.heading: Interstate merger transaction
children.label: 6 Interstate merger transaction
children.text: The term “interstate merger transaction” means any merger transaction approved pursuant to subsection (a)(1).

children.heading: Merger transaction
children.label: 7 Merger transaction
children.text: The term “merger transaction” has the meaning determined under .section 1828(c)(3) of this title

children.heading: Out-of-State bank
children.label: 8 Out-of-State bank
children.text: The term “out-of-State bank” means, with respect to any State, a bank whose home State is another State.

children.heading: Out-of-State bank holding company
children.label: 9 Out-of-State bank holding company
children.text: The term “out-of-State bank holding company” means, with respect to any State, a bank holding company whose home State is another State.

children.heading: Responsible agency
children.label: 10 Responsible agency
children.text: The term “responsible agency” means the agency determined in accordance with with respect to a merger transaction.section 1828(c)(2) of this title

children.heading: Resulting bank
children.label: 11 Resulting bank
children.text: The term “resulting bank” means a bank that has resulted from an interstate merger transaction under this section.
